hints.nominative ('what?'): 'Quid' can be nominative 'what,' but here it functions adverbially meaning 'why'
hints.ablative: Ablative of 'quid' would be 'quo' — 'quid' here is adverbial accusative

hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'vocavistis' — 'vocatis' is present indicative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'vocetis' — 'vocatis' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'vocabitis' — 'vocatis' is present indicative

hints.volo, velle, volui, —: These are the parts of 'velle' (to wish) — irregular verb
hints.voveo, vovere, vovi, votum: These are the parts of 'vovere' (to vow)
hints.volo, volare, volavi, volatum: These are the parts of 'volare' (to fly)

hints.ablative: 'Me' can be ablative, but here it is direct object of 'vocatis'
hints.dative: Dative would be 'mihi' — 'me' is accusative object

steps.reveal: Domine = Lord! (voc., direct address, repeated for emphasis)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Dominus' — 'Domine' with -e is vocative of 2nd declension

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'Domini' ends in -i

hints.to happen: 'To happen' is 'fieri' (passive of 'facere') — active 'facere' means to do/make

hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'fecistis' — 'facitis' is present indicative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'faciatis' — 'facitis' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'facietis' — 'facitis' is present indicative

hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the parts of 'fugere' (to flee)
hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the parts of 'ferre' (to carry)
hints.fio, fieri, factus sum: These are the parts of 'fieri' (to become) — passive of 'facere'

steps.prompt: What is the function of 'quae' in 'quae dico'?
steps.target: relative pronoun (neut. acc. pl.): 'the things which I say'
choices: relative pronoun (neut. acc. pl.): 'the things which I say'
steps.reveal: quae dico = the things which I say

hints.interrogative pronoun: 'what things?': This is not a question — 'quae' refers back to unstated antecedent (the things)
hints.indefinite pronoun: 'some things': 'Quae' as indefinite follows 'si/nisi' — here it is a relative pronoun
hints.conjunction: 'because': 'Quae' is not a conjunction — it is a relative pronoun introducing 'quae dico'

translation: Why do you call me 'Lord, Lord,' and do not do the things which I say?
